# Grey-Source Benchmarks

Grey sources are useful for generating hypotheses. They should not be treated as universal rules.

## Metricool: State of Short-Form Video in Social Media in 2025

URL: https://metricool.com/social-media-short-video-report-2025/

Key points used:

- Metricool analysed over 5 million videos and 582,000 accounts worldwide.
- The report covers short-form video across Reels, TikToks, and Shorts.

Research implication:

```text
Short-form video is saturated enough that creative testing matters more than generic best practices.
```

## Socialinsider: 2025 Social Media Video Performance Statistics

URL: https://www.socialinsider.io/social-media-benchmarks/social-media-video-statistics

Key points used:

- Smaller accounts often had higher engagement rates.
- Instagram Reels between 60 and 90 seconds showed strong engagement and views in their dataset.
- Two-minute TikToks showed strong engagement and views in their dataset.

Research implication:

```text
Do not assume ultra-short is always best. Test idea density and time-to-payoff, not length alone.
```

## Buffer: Engagement in 2025

URL: https://buffer.com/resources/average-engagement-rate/

Key points used:

- Buffer reported YouTube Shorts engagement catching up to TikTok in its dataset.
- The broader implication is that platform differences matter; do not treat all short-form channels as identical.

Research implication:

```text
Post platform-native variants, not blind cross-posts.
```

## Hootsuite: Social video metrics

URL: https://blog.hootsuite.com/social-video-metrics/

Key points used:

- Important social video metrics include view count, watch time, engagement rate, completion rate, CTR, shares, and saves.

Research implication:

```text
Views alone are insufficient. Track watch time, completion, saves, shares, comments, follows, and search traffic.
```

## How to use grey sources

```text
Grey-source claim
→ convert into a hypothesis
→ test on your niche
→ keep only if your own analytics agree
```

Example:

```text
Claim: 60–90s Reels performed strongly in a benchmark.
Hypothesis: 60–90s Reels outperform 15–30s Reels when the idea has enough proof/story density.
Test: same concept in 30s, 60s, 90s.
Metric: completion, saves, sends, follows per 1,000 views.
```
